identity.notes: Agilityの現行Solutionsページ、Digitページ、公式プレスリリースを確認。旧世代や他社機の数値は流用していない。

positioningFacts.statement: Agility positions Digit as a fully autonomous tool with proven commercial deployments for manufacturing, logistics and distribution environments.
Digit is designed for existing human facilities and has a 35 pound carrying capacity, four hour battery life and continuous-shift capability.
Arc is Agility's cloud automation and fleet-management platform, connecting Digit with AMRs and WMS/MES/WES/PLC systems.

mobility.rawValue: Bipedal Mobile Manipulation Robot / general-purpose humanoid with two legs
mobility.evidenceSummary: 公式GXO発表がDigitをbipedal Mobile Manipulation Robotと表記し、公式導入記事もtwo legsを明記。

identity.notes: 現行Atlas製品ページ、2025-12-23公式spec sheet、製品版発表を確認。旧油圧研究Atlasの値は流用していない。

positioningFacts.statement: Boston Dynamics positions Atlas as an enterprise-grade industrial humanoid for autonomous material handling, barcode/RFID workflow integration and order fulfillment.
The current product version is fully electric, supports autonomous, VR-teleoperated and tablet operating modes, and integrates with MES/WMS through Orbit.
Boston Dynamics says Atlas deployments are committed for 2026 with Hyundai and Google DeepMind, with additional customers planned for 2027.

mobility.condition: official product-version announcement calls it a humanoid and describes autonomous navigation

identity.variant: Phoenix Generation 8 current wheeled-base platform; historical Generation 6 numeric sheet reviewed separately
identity.notes: Sanctuary公式はPhoenix Gen8を現行として説明し、Gen6ページには5'7"・155lb・55lb・3mph・20 hand DoFがある。世代差が明確なためGen6数値をGen8へ流用していない。

positioningFacts.statement: Sanctuary presents Phoenix as a general-purpose AI humanoid robot powered by Carbon, its cognitive architecture and software platform.
Phoenix Generation 8 uses a wheeled base, improved telemetry/vision/audio systems and a design optimized for manufacturing and high-quality data capture.
Sanctuary's current strategy deploys Physical AI on industrial robots today while preparing industrial humanoid embodiments; Carbon supports autonomous policies, teleoperation and fleet management.

usageExamples.title: Sanctuary AI Deploys First Humanoid General-Purpose Robot Commercially
usageExamples.summary: Canadian Tire傘下Mark's店舗で1週間のpilotを実施し、品出し・梱包・清掃・タグ付け・ラベリング等110件の小売タスクを検証。Phoenix世代は明記されないため、Phoenix platformの歴史的事例としてvariant不確定を保持。

conflicts: Phoenix generations differ materially: Gen6 official page gives 5'7", 155lb, 55lb maximum payload, 3mph and 20 hand DoF; Gen8 official page has a wheeled base and does not publish those numbers. Historical Gen6 values are intentionally excluded from current fields/loadRatings.
humanReviewRequired: Confirm whether the public robot record should represent Phoenix Gen8 specifically or the multi-generation Phoenix platform; obtain current Gen8 datasheet and Japan support terms before numeric publication.

identity.variant: generic Kaleido record covering multiple generations; RHP7 is the latest variant with a complete public numeric page found
identity.notes: 川崎公式の2026年系譜記事とRHP7公式記事を確認。Kaleidoは世代ごとに数値が異なり、単一世代へ固定できない。

positioningFacts.statement: Kawasaki describes Kaleido as a humanoid robot under research and development, aiming for future practical application and robust fall tolerance.
The Kaleido lineage uses bipedal locomotion and two arms for human-oriented work, with research extending to remote operation for hazardous/disaster environments.
The RHP7 article reports 180cm/80kg and a 4km/h walking speed, while the lineage article reports different historical values for earlier generations.

mobility.rawValue: Kaleido lineage is a bipedal humanoid; generic robot record spans multiple generations
mobility.condition: variant must be fixed before a single enum is displayed
mobility.evidenceSummary: 二足歩行は公式確認できるが、genericレコードの世代固定が未完了。

heightCm.evidenceSummary: 世代別の公式身長を単一値へ統合していない。

weightKg.condition: official generation-specific values conflict for generic record
weightKg.evidenceSummary: 世代別の公式重量を単一値へ統合していない。

speedMps.evidenceSummary: RHP7の4km/hは確認したが、generic値へ流用していない。

positioningFacts.statement: NEURA positions 4NE1 as a cognitive humanoid teammate for unstructured industrial workflows, logistics, manufacturing and everyday assistance.
The current page lists 180cm height, 80kg weight, 5km/h speed and a 10-100kg payload range, and mentions remote operation and exchangeable forearms.

NEURA's 2025 product announcement describes a dual-battery system for uninterrupted 24/7 operation, safe autonomous work and lifting up to 100kg; these claims are not collapsed into a single scalar payload.

identity.variant: First-generation IRON (2024) and Next-Gen IRON (2025) both appear in official sources; current public identity is not fixed to one variant
identity.notes: 2024公式AI Dayは60超の関節・200 DoF超・自社工場/店舗内利用、2025 Next-Genは全身82 DoF・手22 DoF・3 Turing AI chips・3000 TOPS・全固体電池・SDK開放を明記。世代差を分離。

positioningFacts.statement: XPENG positions IRON as a humanoid robot in its full-stack Physical AI system, sharing technology origins with XPENG AI vehicles.

The 2025 Next-Gen IRON is designed for commercial scenarios such as guided tours, shopping guidance and traffic diversion, with Baosteel named as an ecosystem partner for industrial inspection exploration.
XPENG announced that it would open the IRON SDK and build an application ecosystem with global developers.

conflicts: IRON has two official generations: 2024 reports more than 200 DoF, while 2025 Next-Gen reports 82 whole-body/22 hand DoF; 2025 reports 3000 TOPS and a 2026 Singapore release reports 2250 TOPS. No generation is silently selected for the generic record.
